response: Dr. Shelby is credentialed with most major insurance plans, including Medicare, Medi-Cal, Health Net, Cigna, Blue Shield, and Blue Cross. Our staff can verify your specific plan.

Dr. Shelby is credentialed with most major insurance plans, including Medicare, Medi-Cal, Health Net, Cigna, Blue Shield, and Blue Cross. Coverage can vary by plan, so our staff can verify your specific insurance.
00811 is an anesthesia code often associated with colonoscopy. Coverage depends on your plan and whether the colonoscopy is preventive vs diagnostic.
Many colonoscopy/anesthesia codes do not require prior authorization, but some HMO/IPA plans require medical-group authorization. We verify this per plan.
If your plan is HMO/IPA-based, authorization may need to go through your medical group (e.g., John Muir/Hills). We can guide you once we confirm plan details.
If you’re calling an insurer, always request a call reference number. That helps if benefits information later conflicts.
Authorization approval doesn’t determine your out-of-pocket. We still need to check benefits with the insurer (e.g., Cigna) for copay/deductible/coinsurance.
45380 is a colonoscopy code commonly used when biopsies are involved; coverage depends on whether it’s coded preventive vs diagnostic under your plan.
We can rerun benefits, but deductible/out-of-pocket may change with calendar year. If your plan resets in January, timing can affect your cost.
Some insurers describe screening frequency as every 10 years average-risk, and more frequent intervals for high-risk. Your recommended interval depends on your findings and history.
HSA/HRA plan structures can change cost-sharing. We can verify deductible/out-of-pocket but final payment is determined when claims are processed.
We can check eligibility/benefits via Availity/insurer systems, but final payment depends on claims processing and plan terms.
Some plans cover preventive colonoscopy once per calendar year, but frequency can still depend on medical necessity and plan terms.
We can provide benefit estimates, but coverage/payment is determined by your plan when claims are processed. We’ll verify and keep you updated.
Some insurers limit how many patients can be checked per call during high call volume. Get a reference number and call back for additional patients if needed.
Common procedure codes include colonoscopy codes and endoscopy codes; exact codes depend on your indication and plan. For insurance, we can provide codes after confirming your scheduled procedure.
We can rerun benefits, but insurer quotes can differ. We’ll verify closer to the procedure date and update you with the most accurate estimate available.
We typically verify benefits closer to the procedure date (often 1–2 weeks before) when insurers have the most current eligibility/accumulations.
If your deductible isn’t met, out-of-pocket can be higher. Some patients choose to move procedures based on deductible timing—call us and we’ll discuss options.
We can re-run and verify your benefits. Final patient responsibility depends on plan details and preventive vs diagnostic coding.
We typically do not accept Alameda Alliance/CHCN as the medical group/IPA. If possible, ask your PCP/plan about switching to an accepted IPA or consider an alternate GI office.
Anthem PPO plans are generally accepted, but benefits vary. Please send your card photos so we can verify.
Blue Cross Blue Shield PPO plans are generally accepted, but benefits vary. Please send your card photos so we can verify.
Blue Shield DPO plans are often accepted, but coverage varies. Please text the front/back of your card so we can verify benefits and any out-of-pocket.
CareFirst BCBS plans may require authorization depending on codes and setting. We can verify benefits/authorization once we have member details.
If your plan’s IPA/medical group is Brown & Toland, we may be out-of-network depending on the plan setup. Please send your card so we can verify and advise alternatives.
If your HMO is through Canopy Health/John Muir, authorization questions may route through the medical group. Please send your card and we’ll verify next steps.
For HMO plans, we generally accept Hills Physician Medical Group as the IPA/medical group. Please text photos of the front/back of your insurance card so we can confirm.
For HMO plans, we generally accept John Muir Physicians Network as the IPA/medical group. Please text photos of the front/back of your insurance card so we can confirm.
We generally do not accept Medi-Cal only. If you have Medicare plus a secondary managed plan, call us so we can verify.
PHCS/MultiPlan plans are typically not accepted. We can discuss self-pay options if needed.
UnitedHealthcare Medicare PPO plans vary. We can verify benefits and out-of-pocket prior to the procedure—please send your card photos.
UMR/United plans vary by employer group. Please send your insurance card photos so we can verify preventive vs diagnostic coverage.
Medicare supplement choices depend on your needs and network. We can confirm which plans we commonly see, but final selection is your decision with your insurer/broker.
If your IPA/medical group changed, your in-network status may change. Send your updated insurance card and we’ll verify before rescheduling.
If your insurance is changing soon, tell us the effective date. We can try to schedule needed procedures before the change and help you plan the next insurance verification.
We can take some HMO plans, but acceptance depends on your assigned IPA/medical group. Please send your insurance card (front/back) and we’ll verify.
Once you receive our text confirmation, please reply to that text with photos of the front and back of your insurance card (and Medicare card if applicable).

For HMO plans, we generally accept only specific medical groups/IPAs (e.g., John Muir Physicians Network or Hills Physician Medical Group). Some IPAs (like Brown & Toland) may be out-of-network for us.
We generally do not accept Medi-Cal only. If you have Medicare plus a managed secondary plan, please call so we can verify eligibility.
PHCS/MultiPlan plans are typically not accepted. We can discuss self-pay options if needed.
If your plan’s IPA/medical group is not accepted, your out-of-pocket can be very high. Call us with the front/back of the card and we’ll confirm whether your IPA is compatible.
eviCore handles certain Cigna authorizations (often for endoscopy). We can confirm authorization status, but out-of-pocket is determined by Cigna benefits.
We do not accept workers’ compensation cases.
